title: scanAttempts missing composite indexes break Scan Logs UI
description: Review of scan-log feature (0d42f12) found firestore.indexes.json only has single-equality+at indexes for scanAttempts. Shipped queries combine equalities: teacher StudentScanLogsTab count query is outcome==+uid==+at-range -> needs (uid,outcome,at), missing -> FAILED_PRECONDITION 500 on tab load. scanId filter has no index. Add composites: (uid,outcome,at),(classKey,outcome,at),(uid,classKey,outcome,at),(scanId,at),(uid,classKey,at). MANUAL TAIL: firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes after merge.
acceptance_criteria: 5 composite indexes added to firestore.indexes.json; deployed; teacher Scan Logs tab + admin scanId filter load without 500
close_reason: 5 composite indexes added + deployed (firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes succeeded); all 8 scanAttempts indexes live

title: Enable Blaze + Firestore TTL for scanAttempts (30d purge)
description: Decision: upgrade botty-495408 to Blaze, enable Firestore TTL on scanAttempts.expiresAt (already coded in log-repo.ts). Without purge, collection grows unbounded; on Spark the 20K writes/day free cap is the binding wall (scan logging burns 1 write/attempt: ~10K/day at 500 students = 50% of cap; ~20K/day at 1000 students = whole cap -> scans fail; storage also crosses 1GiB at high end). Blaze keeps free daily allowances -> 30d retention cost ~pennies/mo (300K writes/mo under free 20K/day; TTL deletes ~$0.06; storage ~$0.05). MANUAL TAIL: Blaze upgrade + TTL policy enable are console actions, cannot be done from code.
acceptance_criteria: Project on Blaze; TTL policy active on scanAttempts.expiresAt; CLAUDE.md post-deploy note updated; firebase-spark-tier memory superseded
close_reason: Blaze enabled + TTL policy active on scanAttempts.expiresAt (confirmed via firestore:indexes fieldOverride ttl:true); TTL override added to firestore.indexes.json to protect from --force

title: Fix scan resubmit on slow networks: idempotency + upload timeout
description: Slow-network devices cause two failures: (1) Double-award/double-submit -- scanId is server-generated so a retried/concurrent POST gets a fresh id and re-awards points (cooldown only partially masks it; concurrent double-tap races past the cooldown read). (2) Stuck on 'uploading' -- client fetch has no timeout/AbortController, so a slow upload hangs forever with camera already stopped and no retry path.
design: A) Idempotency key: client generates scanId (ulid) once per captured photo, sends in form. Server validates; falls back to server ulid if absent (back-compat). Before cooldown/detect, if scans/{scanId} exists and uid matches -> replay stored ScanResult (no re-award, skip detector+upload). Defense-in-depth: awardScan does tx.get(scanRef) inside the txn and skips writes if doc exists, returning awarded=false -> closes concurrent race. B) Upload timeout: api.ts adds AbortController ~45s (< maxDuration 60) -> typed timeout error. scan/page.tsx keeps captured blob+scanId, shows a slow-connection retry state that re-POSTs the same blob+scanId (safe due to idempotency).
close_reason: Added client-generated scanId idempotency key + transactional create-if-absent (no double-award on retry/concurrent submit) and 45s upload timeout with a slow-connection resend path.

title: Gamification Phase 1 — Earn & Swap MVP
description: Execute docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-05-gamification-phase1.md (18 TDD tasks): coin economy, catalog+achievement gates, purchase/headline APIs, 2.5D tree render, shop UI, home + class-forest display. Subagent-driven on branch feat/gamification-phase1.
notes: Phase 1 implemented on feat/gamification-phase1 (17 commits, 58326d0..a6ee73c). 18 tasks done. 161 tests green, tsc+lint clean. Final review APPROVED_WITH_NITS (opus): dual-currency invariant intact, txns atomic+replay-safe, milestones idempotent. Nits deferred: goal_half gate unused by any item (deliberate, 2 gated trees shipped); /me runs no-op milestone txn per read (acceptable). Build prerender of /admin/scan-logs fails locally only due to empty NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_* (sensitive env not pulled) — not a regression; builds on Vercel.

title: Redesign + fix admin Scan Logs page
description: Scan Logs page (src/app/admin/scan-logs) uses raw inline styles, default fonts, ignores forest theme. Redesign to refined forest dashboard (Kanit display, IBM Plex Sans Thai body, mono IDs, sticky header, polished chips/table). Also fix a behavior bug (symptom TBD from user).
notes: Root cause of frozen counts + 'query'/'kpi' text: scanAttempts/users composite indexes not deployed (or still building) -> count/KPI queries 500 with opaque jsonError tokens; api client surfaces token verbatim; ScanLogTable kept stale data on error so counts looked frozen. CODE FIX: clear data on error + humanizeError(). ACTUAL unfreeze = ops: firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes (firestore.indexes.json already has every needed combo). Also redesigned page UI (forest dashboard).

title: Fix 14 pre-existing ESLint errors (react-hooks/purity, etc.)
description: npm run lint reports 14 errors + 12 warnings, all pre-existing. Main rule: react-hooks/purity 'Cannot call impure function during render' — new Date()/Date.now() called in component bodies (SheetsExportModal.tsx:15, ScanLogTable.tsx:31-32, others). Next 16 'next build' runs ESLint and fails on errors -> builds/deploys may be blocked or lint disabled in build config. Fix: move impure date init into useState initializer fn or useMemo/useEffect. Also: role-guard.ts unused 'required' param, log.ts unused eslint-disable directives.
acceptance_criteria: npm run lint exits 0 (or only warnings); next build passes lint
close_reason: Cleared all 14 ESLint errors (lint exits 0). Corrected premise: Next 16 does NOT run lint during build, so these never blocked deploys — was a CI/quality cleanup. ~half real fixes (lazy useState init, role-guard arg, dead directives, unused pct), ~half documented suppressions of false-positives on SSR-safe / retry-reused effects. Caught+reverted a regression where dropping load() sync prelude would've broken retry buttons (no page test coverage). 1 img warning left (out of scope). 137/137 tests + tsc clean.

title: Scan-log aggregate chips ignore outcome filter
description: countScanAttemptsByOutcome (log-repo.ts:81) always counts all 8 outcomes; route.ts:44 drops q.outcomes when forwarding to count. When admin filters table to one outcome, chips still show full breakdown -> table and chips disagree. Decide: intentional always-on breakdown (document it) or pass outcome filter through. Not in current scope.
acceptance_criteria: Behavior documented as intentional, OR outcome filter forwarded to count query
close_reason: Chips confirmed intentional scope-breakdown (not a bug); made clickable as outcome filter with active-state + clarifying hint; documented in countScanAttemptsByOutcome

title: Enable Firestore TTL on scanAttempts.expiresAt
description: Post-deploy step gating scan-log retention. See AGENTS.md > Post-deploy: enable Firestore TTL. Without this, scanAttempts collection grows unbounded. Blocks closing botty-437.
close_reason: Skipped: project on Spark (free tier), Firestore TTL requires Blaze. Unbounded growth on free tier — at 20 scans/day × 1000 students × 30d ≈ 600k docs ≈ 300MB (under 1GiB free limit but tight). Followup tracked separately.

title: Detector undercount: 21 bottles in single photo → itemCount=1
description: src/server/scan/detect.ts trusts count_objects.output from Roboflow workflow + fallback to preds count. Real-world photo w/ 21 bottles returned itemCount=1. Likely Roboflow workflow config (count_objects block misconfigured) or class filter rejecting most preds. Action: inspect Roboflow workflow output for a known multi-bottle photo, verify count_objects.output value vs preds.length. If count_objects returns 1 always → fix workflow config. If preds drop bottles → relax classMatches() or expand bottleClass env var.

title: Audit Vercel Blob usage
description: Scan for blob put/del/list/get calls. Check access mode (public/private), cleanup paths, orphan handling, and size limits.
notes: Only put used. Findings: no del() anywhere (orphan growth on scan delete), no size cap before put, access:public + no signed URLs, httpsUrl is dead pass-through post-GCS migration.
close_reason: Audit complete, findings reported. Follow-up tickets needed for individual fixes.

title: Audit Firestore query cost (pagination/limits)
description: Scan all .collection() queries for missing limit/pagination, full scans, and N+1 patterns. Report hotspots.
notes: Hotspots: teacher/exports/sheet (no limit, N+1 getUser), kpis student loop (use AggregateField.sum), sevenDaySeries 7 sequential queries, classes full scan, roleRequests pending unbounded. Properly paginated: listMyScans, leaderboard, admin-list, role-changes.
close_reason: Audit complete, findings reported. Follow-up tickets needed for individual fixes.

title: Audit/migrate Firestore scans with gs:// imagePath
description:
Query scans collection for documents where imagePath starts with gs://. Three options per row:
1. Migrate: re-upload image to Vercel Blob, update imagePath to blob URL.
2. Delete: drop scan record (and orphaned image) if user-acceptable.
3. Leave: keep with broken image until row aged out.

Decide policy first. Once zero gs:// rows remain, unblocks dropping legacy fallback (botty-9ot).

Reference: src/server/scan/storage.ts:19-26 legacy branch.
notes:
Audit script written: scripts/audit-gs-paths.mjs. Default dry-run prints count + sample. Flags --delete or --migrate.

Blocked: vercel env pull returns empty for GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON (Vercel masks secrets). User must paste creds into .env.local then run:
  node --env-file=.env.local scripts/audit-gs-paths.mjs

--migrate path not viable since old GCS bucket has billing disabled (image bytes unreachable). Effective options: --delete legacy rows, or leave them (httpsUrl fallback shows broken thumbs).
close_reason: Deleted 18 legacy gs:// scan rows (2026-05-17 to 2026-05-20). Verified zero remain. Points already in user totals untouched. Unblocks botty-9ot.

title: Drop dead GCS_BUCKET env var

Keep: GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON, GCP_PROJECT — still used by Firebase Admin + Sheets API.
close_reason: Removed GCS_BUCKET from Vercel production env via vercel env rm. Preview env already had no such var (env_not_found). Code already clean. Historical mention in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-line-auth-sdk-design.md:94 left as snapshot.

close_reason: Renamed gcsPath -> imageUrl in src/app/api/v1/scan/upload/route.ts. Also updated console.error label from 'gcs upload error' to 'blob upload error'. Typecheck + 115 tests pass.

Clean via console delete, or 'firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes --force' ONLY after confirming firestore.indexes.json mirrors all intended indexes+fieldOverrides (TTL override now in file).
